seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sidmouth and Simpsons Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sidmouth vs Simpsons Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sidmouth and Simpsons Bay.
heading: Sidmouth vs Simpsons Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sidmouth, Tasmania and Simpsons Bay, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Simpsons Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Sidmouth (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sidmouth or Simpsons Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sidmouth has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Simpsons Bay scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sidmouth scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Simpsons Bay scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sidmouth or Simpsons Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Simpsons Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
